metadata.description: A single IP address as its own record (RFC 8344 / NMstate form) — `address` + `allocation` origin — bound to the interface it is configured on. A static DHCP reservation is just `allocation: static` on this record (ADR-023).

context.purpose: Makes a single IP address its own record — origin, interface binding, and allocation — so each address fact lives in exactly one place.
context.plain_description: One IP address, bound to the interface it is configured on, with how it came to be: static (a fixed reservation — this IS the DHCP reservation; there is no second record), leased via DHCP, or self-assigned (SLAAC/privacy). A static record authors the address up front; a dynamic one gets its address filled in once observed. From this one record, projections are derived — a DHCP scope's reservation list, name-side entries — instead of the same fact being retyped per system.
use_when: You need address assignments tracked per interface with their origin (static, dhcp, auto).
You need one authoritative record that DHCP reservation lists and other projections derive from.
not_for: The range addresses come from — Network.IPAddressPool.
The subnet's DHCP service configuration — Network.DHCPScope.
The interface itself — Hardware.NetworkInterface; the address attaches to it.
works_with: Hardware.NetworkInterface — the interface the address is configured on.
Network.IPAddressPool — the pool the address was carved from.
Compute.VirtualMachine — consumers that request or bring addresses.

address.description: The address with prefix as CIDR (RFC 8344/NMstate), e.g. 192.0.2.10/24. Intent for a static reservation; populated at Realized for a dhcp/auto address.

allocation.description: Origin discriminator (RFC 8344 `origin` = NMstate). `static` = a fixed reservation bound to the interface (== a static DHCP reservation); `dhcp` = leased; `link-layer`/`random` = SLAAC/privacy.

relationships.name: attaches_to
relationships.description: attaches_to — the interface this address is configured on. Previously carried as an estate-invented 'assigned_to' note; named from TOSCA AttachesTo when relation names were adopted from standards (common-elements §9).

relationships.name: allocated_from
relationships.description: The pool this address was carved from (mirrors Storage.Dataset.pool → Storage.Pool). Present when the address is allocated from a Network.IPAddressPool rather than statically authored; a realized fact the provider populates, completing allocation-ownership.

adopts.role: relation vocabulary — attaches_to address-on-interface relation name (common-elements §9)
convergent addressing form (address + prefix-length + enabled/dhcp/autoconf) — NetworkManager declarative desired-state
